title: Safe House for Street Girls
tagline: A safe refuge for street girls and their children — including second-generation street children — offering shelter, care, and a path forward.
intro_p1: Girls on the streets face unique and heightened risks — exploitation, gender-based violence, and vulnerability to abuse. For many, the streets are the only option when home is unsafe or non-existent. UCDP's Safe House was established to provide these girls with a dignified, secure, and nurturing alternative.
intro_p2: Our Safe House provides shelter for girls rescued from the streets or referred through our street outreach programme. Importantly, the Safe House also accommodates girls together with their children — because one of the core reasons the Safe House was established was the reality of a second generation of children being born on the streets. We do not separate mothers from their babies.
intro_p3: Residents receive holistic support: safe shelter, nutritious meals, trauma counselling, healthcare access, and pathways into education or vocational training — for both the girls and their children.

impact_cards.label: Protecting Children Born on the Streets

quick_facts.label: Who We Shelter
quick_facts.value: Street girls, street girls and their children

steps.title: Intake
steps.text: Girls are referred through our street outreach team or partner organisations. Mothers with children are welcomed together — we never separate them.

steps.title: Safe Shelter
steps.text: Residents are provided with a safe room, meals, hygiene products, and a welcoming community environment.

steps.title: Counselling & Healing
steps.text: Every resident has access to trauma-informed counselling to help process their experiences and begin healing.

steps.title: Education & Future Planning
steps.text: Residents are supported to re-enter school, join vocational training, or pursue other pathways toward independence — for themselves and their children.
